title: Best Ball 2026 Decision Tree (Contract 3)
description: Schema for the pick-decision tree produced by engine/decision_tree.py (Contract 4) and consumed by the decision dashboard UI (Contract 5). One state + one headline pick + a branching tree of ordered availability scenarios at our pick, each looking ahead 1-2 plies. Branch values come from pick_values (dTitle/dAdv/dW17); branches are ordered best->fallback with the last branch being 'best available / need POS'. From round ~8 on, ties break toward playoff_up (see docs/STRATEGY_SPEC.md S4).

state.description: The live draft state at the moment of this decision.

counts.description: Positional counts on our current roster.

anchor.description: Our best game-stack to build, as 'AWAY@HOME' or 'none' if no stack seeded yet.

headline.description: The single recommended pick at this state.

node.description: A decision node: a label for the pick plus ordered branches (availability scenarios).

label.description: Human label for the pick this node decides, e.g. 'Pick 43 (R4)'.

branches.description: 2-4 branches ordered best->fallback. The LAST branch must be the catch-all 'best available / need POS'.

branch.description: One availability scenario and the pick to make in it, optionally with a 1-2 ply look-ahead subtree.

cond.description: The trigger condition, e.g. 'if Davante Adams (elite WR) still on board'.

take.description: Player to take in this scenario.

dTitle.description: Marginal title-share Delta for this pick.

dAdv.description: Marginal P(advance) Delta for this pick.

dW17.description: Marginal W17 (finals) Delta for this pick.

playoff_up.description: Player's normalized W15-17 ceiling overlay (from engine/playoff_overlay.csv); the late-round tie-breaker/driver.

then.description: Optional next-pick subtree (same node shape), for 1-2 ply look-ahead.

headline.why: Elite-ceiling WR falling past ADP (46.7); adds a third alpha WR to the core and carries a top-tier W15-17 slate (LAR vs DAL/SEA/TB). Advancement-positive AND finals-positive at R4.

branches.cond: if Davante Adams (elite WR) still on board
branches.reason: Best ceiling-volume on the board and value vs MERGED RANK (ADP 46.7, fell to us). WR is the core (target 8-9) and we still need WR depth. Highest playoff_up of the three options (LAR is a top offense with a soft finals slate) -- a tie-breaker here, not the driver yet at R4.

branches.cond: if a lead-back RB2 (e.g. Josh Jacobs) is available
branches.reason: We are RB-light (1). Securing a second clear lead back by R5 is the advancement-insurance rule; carries are bankable W1-14 volume.

branches.cond: else best available WR / need POS
branches.reason: If RB room thins, keep stacking WR ceiling; circle back to RB2 in R6. Fallback = best available.

branches.cond: else if a top-RB (e.g. Josh Jacobs) is the best value
branches.reason: If Adams is gone, pivot to RB: we have only 1 RB and need 2 lead backs by R5. Jacobs is a workhorse (volume = advancement), and GB's playoff environment is solid. Take the floor-securing pick over a lateral WR.

branches.cond: if an alpha WR fell (e.g. DK Metcalf)
branches.reason: Back to the WR core (target 8-9) with a ceiling WR who fell; we now have RB floor secured.
Elite-TE-or-wait: if the alpha TE tier is here at value, take it and we need only one more TE late. Otherwise best available.

branches.cond: else best available / need POS (Adams and the RB tier both gone)
branches.reason: Catch-all fallback. Best ceiling-volume left; WR is never a wasted pick given the 8-9 target. NOTE: Higgins is CIN -> pairs with our Ja'Marr Chase for a same-team seed, but Chase+Higgins are WR-WR (r~0, NOT a true correlated stack) -- treat as depth, not as our anchor; seek the QB+catcher+bring-back anchor later.
